items.final_wording: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

source.title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations
source.population: Adults aged over 18 presenting with hearing difficulties or hearing loss.
source.setting: Primary, community, audiology, and secondary care.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult hearing-difficulty assessment, red-flag referral, otoscopy, audiology, and hearing-support documentation; partial for children and UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.8.1 — audiology assessment, management, and support

source.evidence_paraphrase: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

items.rationale: The exact adult hearing section supports interval hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, audiometry, tympanometry, personalised discussion and reviewed outcomes without recommending a device. The exact assessment section supports clinician-entered plan, follow-up and questions based on reviewed hearing needs and results.

items.final_wording: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

source.title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations
source.population: Adults aged over 18 presenting with hearing difficulties or hearing loss.
source.setting: Primary, community, audiology, and secondary care.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult hearing-difficulty assessment, red-flag referral, otoscopy, audiology, and hearing-support documentation; partial for children and UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.5–1.3.2 — additional symptoms, ear findings, and referral

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

items.rationale: The exact section supports clinician-performed assessment and concern documentation without generating referral.
